sections.paragraph:
an unused root, of the same sense as גָּבַב to be elevated, to rise up, liked a back or hump. For the derivatives which partly follow the analogy of verbs עע֞, partly of verbs לה֞ (גַּו, גַּוִּי; גֵּו for גֵּוֶה, גֵּבָה, גְּוִיָּה), have the signification both of back (see גַּב ) and of belly (see גַּי ), which latter is applied to middle (compare بَطْنُ belly middle, interior, بَاطنُ within). [“From the belly comes then the word for body; see גֵּוָה , גְּוִיָּה and this idea is then transferred to the signification of people, see גֹּוי .” Ges. add.]

II.
